items.title: World Cup 2026 : France battle through Paraguay dark arts
items.description: France is through to the round of 16 after a fierce battle with Paraguay. Paraguay sat in a low block and tried to frustrate les Bleus, often committing bad fouls, all of which went unpunished. Kylian Mbappé scored the only goal of the game, converting a penalty. Morocco were slightly stunned by Canada in the first half of their game in Houston, unfortunately, the Canucks were unable to keep the pressure going and conceded the first goal of the fixture just after half time. Last minute risks did not pay off for the co-hosts who suffered two more goals at the hands of the Atlas Lions. That 3-0 win means that Morocco will face France in the quarter finals, a dramatic remake of the 2022 World Cup.

items.description: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

items.title: Prosecutors to lay out their case against the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk
items.description: The five-day preliminary hearing that starts on Monday (July 6, 2026) will be the first time members of Kirk's family are in the Utah courtroom with defendant Tyler Robinson

items.title: Isaac Del Toro overcomes mechanical setback to win Tour de France 2nd stage
items.description: Isaac del ​Toro persevered, despite a mechanical issue and a prolonged ​wait ‌to ⁠change his bike, to ‌mount a late challenge ⁠and win the second stage of ​the Tour ‌de France in a time trial in Barcelona on Sunday, becoming the ‌second Mexican rider to ​win a stage at the race after Raul ​Alcala.

items.title: British military says cargo ship reports being under attack in Red Sea off coast of Yemen
items.description: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations centre reported the attack off the coastal city of Hodeida, which is under control of the Iranian-backed Houthi rebels

items.title: German coalition agrees on changes to pensions, tax rates
items.description: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government plans to push through sweeping changes with the stated goal of reviving a sluggish economy. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

items.title: Egypt says it is expecting a further $1.7 billion from Europe within days
items.description: It is part a $5.7 billion EU macro-financial assistance package within the framework of Europe’s strategy to stabilise Egypt's economy.

items.title: US to block UN funding for Somalia in blow to AU peace mission
items.description: In a diplomatic note to the African Union seen by Reuters, the US has vowed to block any spending of UN funds on the AU Support ​and Stabilisation Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM).

items.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
items.description: A pastor of a prominent underground church detained in China in October has been released, according to rights advocates

items.title: Eight rescued from seaplane that made 'hard landing' in Manhattan river
items.description: Eight people were removed from the Kodiak 100 seaplane by fire department units and two had minor injures.

items.title: African Union peacekeeping mission in Somalia at risk amid US funding cuts
items.description: Washington said its decision was due the government’s lack of progress in containing the al-Shabaab group or in taking ownership of its own security.

items.title: Iran: Pressure on persecuted Baha'i community intensifies
items.description: Arrests, torture, mock executions: War has seen the Iranian regime crack down on dissidents of all kinds. Members of the country's Baha'i faith have long been persecuted, but their situation is now worsening.

items.title: Australia probes mystery space balls that washed up on beach
items.description: Officials are searching for the origins of six pieces of space debris discovered on Forrest Beach in Queensland.

items.title: Trial of Ebola treatment gets underway in Ituri
items.description: A first ray of hope for Ebola patients in the Democratic Republic of the Congo as the trial of two potential treatments for the virus gets underway.

items.title: MoS External Affairs to lead Indian delegation at UN Dialogue on AI governance
items.description: The dialogue will help to build capacities, especially in developing countries, to direct the AI systems towards the pursuit of sustainable development, says Minister of State for External Affairs Kirti Vardhan Singh

items.title: China's military promotes 2 new generals after anti-corruption purge thins ranks
items.description: The shake-up is believed to be an effort to ensure the military's loyalty to the Communist Party and its leader, Xi Jinping.

items.title: More stars or stripes?
items.description: Within a decade of the Civil War, the US economy had overtaken Great Britain and the entire British Empire by around 1895 – from that point onward, it was more a question of the US impact on world history than the reverse. Leer más

items.title: Cape Verde's historic World Cup ride ends after pushing Argentina to the brink
items.description: Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.

items.title: Over one million undocumented migrants apply for residency in Spain
items.description: The Socialist government's mass regularisation programme, which closed on 30 June, comes as others European countries crack down on irregular immigration.

items.title: Argentina's Nico Paz happy to extend Como loan say Real Madrid
items.description: Real Madrid have given Argentina star Nico Paz permission to extend his loan deal with Italian side Como. Leer más

items.title: Thieves steal around €4 million in jewellery from French museum
items.description: Jewellery worth several million euros has been stolen from the Lalique Museum in northeastern France after burglars broke into the site early on Sunday and targeted its jewellery collection. Around 20 pieces were taken in the raid, with losses estimated at close to four million euros, according to an investigation source.

items.title: Uruguay veteran Edison Cavani departs Boca Juniors
items.description: Uruguayan striker leaves Boca without having won a title and acknowledges he had "difficult moments" that prevented him from "leaving a mark" on the club. Leer más

items.title: Iran holds funeral for Ali Khamenei: Who is attending?
items.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28, 2026, in joint precision strikes by the US and Israeli militaries. The airstrikes killed Khamenei as well as other members of his family.

items.title: Millions attend funeral prayers for Iran’s Khamenei and family
items.description: Millions of people have attended funeral prayers in Tehran for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: South Korea to create future fund from chip windfall to spur growth, tackle inequality
items.description: A top official says the government will use the "Future Response Fund" to help finance major national investment projects and strengthen the country's long-term competitiveness.

items.title: Yvonne Jegede’s friendly ex, Kunle Fawole, reveals strong postmarital love for her
items.description: The love story of both actors was once regarded as one of Nollywood's most enduring friendships that blossomed into marriage before ending in a widely publicised separation. The post Yvonne Jegede’s friendly ex, Kunle Fawole, reveals strong postmarital love for her appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Alibaba bans Claude for staff – Anthropic didn't want them using it anyway
items.description: Chinese tech giant Alibaba has ordered staff to stop using Anthropic's Claude Code, after it was found to be flagging users connecting from China. But Anthropic is already trying its best to stop Chinese firms from using Claude at all, and accuses Alibaba of running large "distillation" campaigns against it, saying it deployed around 25,000 fake accounts to train its own models on Claude.

items.title: West Asia war updates on July 4, 2026: Huge crowds throng Tehran for Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral ceremonies
items.description: Authorities unveiled the casket containing Khamenei's body in a glass case at the Grand Mosalla in Tehran, Iran's capital

items.title: Marcela Pagano: ‘It’s become evident that it is Karina Milei who calls the shots’
items.description: National deputy says Javier Milei’s government "has covered up Manuel Adorni’s corruption," that voters have fallen out of love with La Libertad Avanza and that Karina Milei, the President’s sister, is the one who’s really in charge in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Argentina continue knockout preparations in scorching Kansas City heat
items.description: Lionel Scaloni's side returned to training under an extreme heat warning with three selection questions still to resolve ahead of Friday's last-16 clash against Cape Verde. Leer más

items.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
items.description: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

items.description: Diego Maradona suffered a sudden change in mood that was downplayed by his psychiatrist during the period of home hospitalisation in which he died, claims nurse in testimony. Leer más

items.description: Rescue operations ⁠to search for ⁠survivors are still underway

items.title: Jürgen Klopp and Germany: A partnership long in the making
items.description: In one of the worst-kept secret in football, the former Dortmund and Liverpool coach looks to be on the verge of becoming Germany's new boss.

items.title: SK Hynix considering 0.5% fee payout in ADR offering, Bloomberg News reports
Guam and surrounding Pacific islands brace for impact of Super Typhoon Bavi
items.description: People in the Northern Mariana Islands – remote U.S. territories in the Pacific Ocean – are preparing for Super Typhoon Bavi, which experts say could bring winds of over 180 miles per hour.

items.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
items.description: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.
